summary: Tested the 3 new changes: (1) relative /api base, (2) customizable location-consent modal, (3) global primary_color CSS var. Backend: 21/22 pytest PASS. Added 4 new tests: settings primary_color persistence, job defaults include consent fields, job update persists custom consent labels, public /api/jobs returns consent_* fields. Frontend: admin brand-color save recolored buttons LIVE without page reload (--primary flipped from EA580C to hsl 221 83% 53%, save-btn bg changed from rgb(233,89,12) -> rgba(36,99,235,.9)); New Job dialog defaults verified (3 fields: full_name/email/phone; form_heading='Your Details'; consent switch=checked; default consent_title/body/agree/decline all present); Consent ON path -> modal shows job.consent_title 'TEST Custom Privacy', body 'TEST Custom body...', Agree='OK Share Location', Decline='No Thanks'; Decline closes modal (no submission); Agree triggers geolocation (40.7128,-74.006) + POST /api/checkins with responses[]; checkin-success appears; pin appears; DB confirms 1 check-in with contractor_name='TEST_UI_v4_Alice'. Consent OFF path -> Share Location skips the modal entirely and submits directly. Relative /api works (network calls same-origin succeed). Admin login OK with cookie auth. One pre-existing backend test failure carried over from iteration_3 (test_migration_added_contact_fields) because a previously-edited seed job legitimately lacks 'phone' - not a real bug in this iteration.

minor.issue: test_migration_added_contact_fields fails because a job (id 6a57e6d07976af16733fa8ed) has {email, full_name, id_badge, site_number} - no phone. RCA: iteration_3 admin-edit test removed 'phone' via PUT /api/jobs. The migration only prepends defaults for jobs missing form_heading; it does not re-prepend fields that admins subsequently removed by design. Consider relaxing this assertion to 'full_name in keys and (email in keys or phone in keys)' or seed a fresh DB per test.

action_items: LOW: Loosen test_migration_added_contact_fields assertion in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py so it doesn't fail when an admin has intentionally removed the phone field from a previously-migrated job.
LOW (carry-over from iteration_1/2/3): AdminDashboard.js is 507 lines with 4 inline sub-components (CheckInsTab, JobsTab, JobDialog, SettingsTab) - consider splitting into separate files.
LOW (carry-over from iteration_1): wrap navigator.clipboard.writeText(url) in copyLink with try/catch (currently in AdminDashboard.js line 210).
LOW: JobDialog.updateField still auto-derives key from label without dedupe - two rows labelled 'Full Name' would both get key 'full_name'.
LOW: create_checkin in server.py still uses substring matching ('name' in k / 'email' in k) - could collide with keys like 'company_name'/'emergency_contact_email'. Prefer exact key priority.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:398 - migration only triggers when form_heading is missing (one-shot). Once migrated, admins can freely remove default fields; that's a valid product decision but the corresponding test in backend_test.py needs to be more permissive.

frontend/src/lib/api.js:5 - API = '/api' relative is clean; works when frontend and backend are same-origin behind ingress. On dev servers where they're on different ports, this would break. Fine for the current preview/prod topology.
frontend/src/lib/theme.js:28 - applyPrimaryColor sets --primary and --ring on documentElement.style, which correctly overrides Tailwind's HSL var-based theme. Works without page reload as tested.
AdminDashboard.SettingsTab: primary_color hex Input has no validation - a malformed hex silently no-ops in hexToHslString. Consider showing a warning when the value doesn't match /^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$/.

PrivacyModal.js: uses whitespace-pre-line + max-h-72 overflow-y-auto on body - good for long custom text. Uses `body || ''` default at prop level; PrivacyModal falls back to '' when body is empty (renders empty div). Consider showing a sensible default body if consent_body is blank on a job.

server.py:207 - _set_auth_cookie sets samesite='none' + secure=True, which is required for cross-origin cookie flows. With the frontend now on same-origin '/api', samesite='lax' would be safer, but 'none'+secure is still valid.
server.py CheckInInput.responses default_factory=list - still silently accepts old-shape payloads with no responses. Consider making it required with min_items>=1 (noted in iteration_3).

success_rate.backend: 95.5% (21/22 pytest; the 1 failure is a stale-assertion carry-over, not a real bug)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (login, brand-color live-recolor, new-job defaults, consent ON modal with custom labels, decline closes without submit, agree submits + pin, consent OFF skips modal + submits directly)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_UI_v4_ConsentOn and TEST_UI_v4_ConsentOff jobs during testing; both deleted at end (cascade also removed their check-ins). Pre-existing TEST_Alice and TEST_John check-ins on the Downtown Tower seed job remain from prior iterations (per user preference).
context_for_next_testing_agent: For public check-in flow: page.context.grant_permissions(['geolocation'], origin=BASE) + set_geolocation({latitude:40.7128,longitude:-74.006}) BEFORE clicking share-location-btn. The consent modal is between share-location-btn and geolocation call; when consent_enabled=false, geolocation fires directly. Backend cookie is httpOnly access_token with samesite=none+secure. Frontend API base is now relative ('/api') via /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js. Primary-color CSS var applied on: (a) app boot via ThemeLoader in App.js, (b) after SettingsTab save via applyPrimaryColor. --primary uses 'H S% L%' string format read by tailwind's hsl(var(--primary)).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - all features under review are working correctly.
